The process of pasteurization involves heating a liquid to a specific temperature for a set duration to eliminate harmful microorganisms without significantly altering its composition
In double-entry bookkeeping, every financial transaction is recorded in at least two accounts, with debits on the left side and credits on the right side of the ledger

Wheat flour is classified by its protein content, with bread flour containing twelve to fourteen percent protein and cake flour containing seven to nine percent

Plywood is manufactured by gluing together thin layers of wood veneer with the grain of adjacent layers rotated at right angles to increase structural strength
The term 'jurisdiction' in legal contexts refers to the authority granted to a court or other legal body to hear cases and make binding decisions within a defined geographic or subject-matter area

Stainless steel is an alloy composed primarily of iron, with a minimum chromium content of ten and one-half percent, which gives it resistance to corrosion and staining
A paragraph in formal writing generally contains a topic sentence, supporting sentences that develop the main idea, and a concluding sentence that transitions to the next paragraph
The Richter scale measures the magnitude of an earthquake on a logarithmic basis, meaning each whole number increase represents a tenfold increase in measured amplitude

The Universal Product Code, commonly known as a UPC barcode, consists of twelve numerical digits that uniquely identify a retail product and its manufacturer
A hectare is a metric unit of area equal to ten thousand square meters, or approximately two point four seven acres in the imperial system

The three branches of the United States federal government are the legislative branch, the executive branch, and the judicial branch, each with distinct responsibilities

The diameter of a standard basketball is approximately nine and one-half inches, and the ball weighs between twenty and twenty-two ounces when properly inflated

The term 'quorum' refers to the minimum number of members of an assembly or organization who must be present at a meeting for decisions to be legally valid
Drip irrigation delivers water directly to the root zone of plants through a network of tubes and emitters, reducing water usage by thirty to fifty percent compared to overhead sprinklers
The freezing point of salt water varies depending on salinity, with typical ocean water freezing at approximately minus two degrees Celsius rather than zero
A bill of lading is a legal document issued by a carrier to a shipper that details the type, quantity, and destination of the goods being transported
The ISBN-13 format replaced the older ISBN-10 format in 2007, adding a three-digit prefix to align with the global EAN barcode identification system
Asphalt pavement is composed of approximately ninety-five percent aggregate materials such as crushed stone and sand, bound together by five percent asphalt cement
The term 'amortization' in finance refers to the gradual repayment of a loan through scheduled installments that include both principal and interest components
A compass needle aligns with the Earth's magnetic field, pointing toward magnetic north, which differs from true geographic north by an angle known as magnetic declination
The average typing speed for a professional typist is approximately sixty-five to seventy-five words per minute, measured using standardized five-character word units
Reinforced concrete combines the compressive strength of concrete with the tensile strength of embedded steel bars, allowing it to resist both crushing and stretching forces
The standard paper size known as Letter measures eight and one-half by eleven inches, while the international A4 standard measures two hundred ten by two hundred ninety-seven millimeters
